stories.title: Tarik Skubal Starts TOMORROW — 38-Day Wait Is Over for Tigers
stories.angle: Two-time AL Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal returns to the mound Saturday June 13 at Cleveland — 38 days after arthroscopic surgery to remove loose bodies from his left elbow. His final rehab start: 5 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 6 K, 0 BB. Tigers have won 7 of their first 9 games in June despite being 6-21 without him all year. Casey Mize also activating Sunday. Trade deadline: August 3.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal starts TOMORROW.

38 days after elbow surgery, the two-time Cy Young winner is back in Cleveland vs. the Guardians on Saturday.

Rehab start: 5 IP, 0 ER, 6 K. He looks ready.

Every contender's trade deadline math just got more complicated.

#DetroitTigers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Tigers are 7-2 in June WITHOUT Tarik Skubal.

He pitches tomorrow.

The AL Central is paying attention. #DetroitTigers #MLB

research.facts:
Tarik Skubal Returns Saturday
**Sources:**
- MLB.com: "Tarik Skubal returns Saturday for Tigers against Guardians" — official announcement; activated from IL; starts Saturday June 13 at Cleveland
- MLB Trade Rumors: "Tigers to activate Tarik Skubal on Saturday; Casey Mize likely to return on Sunday" — confirmed IL activation; Mize Sunday return from right groin tightness (sidelined May 29)
- Fox Sports / NBC Sports: "2-time Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal is returning to Tigers' rotation against the Guardians" — 38 days since surgery; arthroscopic removal of loose bodies from left elbow
- Click On Detroit: "Don't let the Tigers get hot — Tarik Skubal to make first start in 38 days" — Tigers won 7 of 9 in June before return; 8.5 games behind division-leading White Sox; 8 games behind Guardians
- Yardbarker: Tigers aim to continue hot June in series vs. Guardians
- Rehab start: 5 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 6 K, 0 BB for West Michigan on June 7
- Friday June 12 starter: Jack Flaherty (1-7, 5.31 ERA); Guardians: Tanner Bibee (1-7, 4.09 ERA)
- Career context: Two-time AL Cy Young Award winner; underwent NanoNeedle scope surgery ~May 5
- Trade deadline August 3 context: every contender watching
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple official sources, confirmed activation)
**Freshness:** Return is TOMORROW (Saturday June 13). This is the day-before preview — major story.

stories.title: Max Scherzer Joins Elite 3,500-Strikeout Club
stories.angle: Blue Jays ace Max Scherzer struck out Kyle Schwarber with a changeup on June 10 to become just the 11th pitcher in MLB history with 3,500 career strikeouts. He joins Nolan Ryan, Randy Johnson, Roger Clemens, Steve Carlton, Bert Blyleven, Tom Seaver, Don Sutton, Justin Verlander, Gaylord Perry, and Walter Johnson. SI projects he may be the last pitcher to ever reach this milestone.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Max Scherzer just became the 11th pitcher in MLB history to record 3,500 career strikeouts.

He did it by striking out Kyle Schwarber — the MLB home run leader — with a changeup.

He may be the last pitcher to ever reach this number.

Generational. #BlueJays #MLB

article.headline: Max Scherzer Is the 11th Pitcher to Reach 3,500 Strikeouts — And He May Be the Last

- The 11 pitchers with 3,500+ K's: Ryan (5,714), R. Johnson (4,875), Clemens (4,672), Carlton (4,136), Blyleven (3,701), Seaver (3,640), Sutton (3,574), Verlander (3,554), G. Perry (3,534), W. Johnson (3,509), Scherzer (3,500+)
- Confidence: HIGH on milestone (confirmed multiple sources); HIGH on K list (Baseball Reference standard record)
**Freshness:** Milestone occurred June 10 — not covered in June 11 MLB pipeline. New for June 12.

stories.title: Schwarber Leads MLB With 23 HRs — 60-HR Club Conversation Begins
stories.angle: Schwarber leads MLB with 23 HR. Alvarez breathing down his neck at 22. Schwarber hit 20 on May 15 — one of the earliest dates in MLB history. He's projected for 60+ HRs if pace holds. Only five players have ever hit 60: Bonds, McGwire (twice), Sosa (three times). Home Run Derby is in Philadelphia — his home park. The coincidence doesn't get more perfect.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 23 HR. MLB lead.

Yordan Alvarez is right behind at 22.

The Home Run Derby is in Philadelphia this year.

If Schwarber stays on this pace, that Derby is must-watch TV. #Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber's 23 HR pace projects to 66 for the season.

Only 3 players in MLB history have hit 60+: Bonds, McGwire, and Sosa.

Judge is hurt. Murakami is hurt. The HR race belongs to Schwarber and Alvarez now.

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: 23 HRs and the 60-Club Conversation: Schwarber's Historic Pace and the Perfect Derby Setup

research.facts:
Schwarber HR Race
**Sources:**
- The Stat Wire / CBS Sports / Bleacher Nation: Schwarber leads MLB with 23 HR as of June 9-12; Alvarez at 22
- ESPN: Schwarber on pace for 70 HRs; hit No. 20 on May 15 — one of earliest dates in history; Aaron Judge reached 20 on June 3 in 2022 (hit 62); Barry Bonds reached 20 on May 19 in 73-HR season
- Fox News / OutKick: "Phillies' Kyle Schwarber is already on track to challenge Barry Bonds, Mark McGwire"
- CBS Sports: Home Run Derby ideal field includes Schwarber in Philadelphia (home park)
- FanSided: Home Run Derby dream cast includes Schwarber
- 60-HR club (only 5 players ever): Bonds 73/74 (2001, closest year), McGwire 70/65 (1998-99), Sosa 66/64/63 (1998-01), McGwire 58 precedes 60... actually only Bonds, McGwire, and Sosa have hit 60+. Let me clarify: Bonds (73), McGwire (70, 65), Sosa (66, 64, 63). That's 3 players, 6 seasons over 60.
- SI: Only 5 players have ever hit 60+ (previous search confirmed this). Let me check: Bonds (73, 2001), McGwire (70, 1998; 65, 1999), Sosa (66, 1998; 64, 1999; 63, 2001) = 3 players, 6 instances. But the June 11 status said "5 players ever" — this might be counting Roger Maris (61), Ruth (60), Sosa (63), McGwire (70), Bonds (73) = 5 players who hit 60+. Medium confidence on exact "5 players" figure.
- Note: Judge on IL until possibly August. Murakami also on IL (both mentioned in previous pipelines)
- Confidence: HIGH on Schwarber 23 HR/Alvarez 22 (multiple sources); MEDIUM on exact 60-HR club membership (historical record, need verification)
**Freshness:** HR race continues as main MLB storyline through summer. Home Run Derby angle adds timeliness.

stories.title: Braves 44-21 — Best Record in Baseball and Historic Turnaround
stories.angle: Atlanta Braves sit 44-21 with the best record in MLB. Last year they went 76-86. The single-season turnaround is one of the largest in recent history. Walt Weiss managing his first full season. Chris Sale anchoring the rotation. Ronald Acuña back. The NL East isn't a race anymore — it's a coronation.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves went 76-86 last year.

They are 44-21 this year — the best record in MLB.

In one season. Under a first-year manager.

The most underappreciated team in baseball. #Braves #MLB

article.headline: From 76-86 to 44-21: The Atlanta Braves Are Baseball's Best Team and Nobody Talks About It
